data.title: AGT Food & Ingredients Seen Riding Demand for Nutrient-Rich Foods — Market Talk
data.description: AGT Food & Ingredients is positioned to benefit from "strong structural tailwinds for affordable, nutrient-dense food," says RBC's Ryland Conrad in a note, initiating coverage with an outperform rating and a C$25 price-target. AGT IPOed earlier this month, and the analyst says there's a rising global protein consumption and exposure to high-growth emerging markets which the plant-based ingredients company can tap into. The demand is global, Conrad says, noting that the company has a strong presence in emerging markets, partly through food security contracts. These provide the company exposure to regions experiencing higher population growth and rising food consumption. AGT Food & Ingredients is up 2.2% to C$19.40. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: Chip Stocks Jump as Trump Signals End to Iran War Within Weeks
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks climbed Tuesday following a report that President Donald Trump intends to wind down the military campaign against Iran within weeks.

Nvidia jumped about 3%, and Advanced Micro Devices rose nearly 2%. Broadcom added roughly 3%. Marvell Technology , which is set to receive a $2 billion investment from Nvidia, surged about 8%.

Other networking and AI-related stocks also moved higher. Celestica climbed about 5%, while Coherent (COHR) and Ciena each added roughly 4% and 3%, respectively.

Separately, reports indicated that Samsung Electronics and SK hynix hold four to six months of helium inventory, a gas used in chip manufacturing. Samsung shares fell about 5% in South Korea trading, while SK hynix dropped nearly 8%. Both companies are key memory suppliers, with SK hynix providing high-bandwidth memory chips to Nvidia.

data.title: CalEthos 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.25) EPS on $(6.498M) Net Loss
data.description:
CalEthos reported no revenues for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024 and posted a net loss of $6.498 million, or $(0.25) per share, for 2025; the 2025 results include $4.581 million of abandoned project costs and significant non-cash items that materially affected net income. Operating loss for 2025 was $(1.055) million. The company continues to shift its operational focus toward a master-planned, onsite-powered data center campus with planned regulatory and permitting milestones through 2026–2027.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — No revenues reported for years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Operating Income: $(1.055) million — Loss from operations of $(1,055,000) for 2025.
  • Net Income: $(6.498) million — Net loss of $(6,498,000) for 2025 (includes $4.581 million of abandoned project costs and large non-cash items).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.25) — Net loss per share, basic and diluted, $(0.25) for 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Onsite-powered campus development: Initiated development of a master-planned data center campus focused on onsite behind-the-meter natural gas power and a platform described as PIaaS.
  • Project milestone timing: Targeting land-use and zone change approvals by year-end 2026 and construction permits by Q2 2027.
  • Operational pivot: Abandoned the prior Imperial County project in July 2025, expensing previously capitalized development costs and halting capitalization of that project.
  • Regulatory and environmental workstream: Preparing county and state environmental studies and design filings for an onsite natural gas plant, with filings planned through mid-2026.

Original SEC Filing: CalEthos, Inc. [ GEDC ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nasdaq, Dow jump on peace prospects; Powell pivot fuels Q1 finale
data.description:
Wall Street is set to conclude the first quarter of 2026 with an explosive exclamation point, as investors ignited a massive relief rally on hopes of a geopolitical de-escalation.

In a stunning display of market resilience, the Nasdaq Composite surged by 3.6%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average popped 2.2%, marking a significant turning point for investor sentiment.

The rally was catalyzed primarily by growing optimism that an end to the Iran war may now be in the offing.

Despite the underlying complexity of the conflict, the mere prospect of a “ceasefire” triggered waves of optimism, shifting the narrative from wartime defense back to aggressive accumulation.

Why did US indices push higher on Tuesday?

The primary engine behind Tuesday’s surge was a sudden wave of optimism on the US-Iran conflict.

Markets hit session highs following reports that President Pezeshkian is seeking a diplomatic exit, provided Iran receives reparations and firm security guarantees.

Adding fuel to the rally, the Wall Street Journal and New York Post reported that President Donald Trump expressed a willingness to end military hostilities – suggesting other nations could lead the effort to reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

While Eric Diton of The Wealth Alliance warned that we’re “not out of the woods” yet, the market embraced the “relief rally” wholeheartedly, desperate for any sign that the regional instability may finally be nearing a solution.

The Powell pivot: a green light for growth

Complementing the geopolitical hope was a high-profile address by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell at Harvard University.

Despite the ongoing energy shock caused by Middle East tensions, Powell reassured the markets that the central bank sees no immediate need to hike interest rates further.

By characterizing the current interest rate environment as being in a “good place”, he effectively neutralized fears that surging oil prices – which remain firmly above $100 per barrel – would force the Fed into a restrictive corner.

This wait-and-see approach provided the ultimate oxygen for the tech sector, as lower-than-feared terminal rates traditionally benefit high-growth companies.

Investors responded by pouring capital back into long-duration assets, betting the central bank will prioritize economic stability over reactionary tightening.

AI and enterprise innovation take center stage

With the macro clouds beginning to part, the tech sector – which had been under immense pressure since the conflict began – staged a ferocious comeback.

The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) climbed more than 3%, led by a nearly 5% surge in Nvidia and a 3% gain for Microsoft.

Importantly, this wasn’t just a technical bounce; it was an affirmation of AI’s enduring power as well as enterprise innovation. Investors are increasingly betting that the “AI-first” transition will provide the productivity gains needed to offset higher energy costs.

As companies continue to integrate AI into their core business models, the sector is proving to be the market’s primary growth engine, transforming from a speculative play into a fundamental pillar of the global economy.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $66,700, Eyes First Monthly Gain Since September
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is attempting to steady after months of sustained pressure, holding near $66,700 as March comes to a close and positioning for what could be its first monthly gain since September. The price action follows a five-month slide that wiped more than 40% off its value, but sentiment appears to be shifting as President Donald Trump signals he is weighing an end to the US military campaign against Iran. That shift in geopolitical tone has started to ripple across markets, with S&P 500 futures reversing earlier losses to trade up around 1%, suggesting investors may be reassessing worst-case energy disruption scenarios tied to the Strait of Hormuz.

Across crypto, the response has been constructive but measured, with Ether rising as much as 3.3% while Solana and XRP gained up to 2.2% and 1.5%, respectively. Market participants appear increasingly focused on the possibility that the conflict could end sooner than expected without reopening the Strait, a dynamic that could be helping Bitcoin remain anchored near the $68,000 range. At the same time, technical signals remain mixed, with the token still trading below its 50-day moving average even as it continues to bounce along an upward support line formed over the past two months.

Positioning data suggests investors are not abandoning caution. Deribit shows more than $1.5 billion in put options clustered at the $60,000 level, reflecting ongoing demand for downside protection, although some expect that hedging activity could ease in the coming weeks. Still, Bitcoin's relative resilience stands out, particularly as gold has dropped more than 13% this month while the cryptocurrency has gained roughly 2%. According to Glassnode, easing sell-side pressure and stabilizing flows may be laying the groundwork for a potential recovery, though stronger demand would likely be required to confirm any sustained shift in trend.

data.title: Crude Oil Plunges After Reports Hinting At U.S. Withdrawal From Iran
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Crude oil plummeted on Tuesday after reports indicated that U.S. President Donald Trump was willing to wrap up the U.S. military offensive in Iran and push Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz through diplomatic efforts.

WTI Crude Oil for May delivery was last seen trading down by $1.28 (or 1.24%) at $101.60 per barrel.

Since the start of the U.S.-Israel versus Iran war on February 28, the Strait of Hormuz, which is a critical chokepoint for oil exports from Arab nations to the rest of the world, remains closed.

Several oil tankers are still stranded at the sea with no way to reach their destinations.

With transit crippled and storage difficulties increasing, a few Arab countries halted oil production and some scaled down the output drastically. The combined effect of production cuts and transit disruption has pushed oil prices higher.

As a consequence, inflationary concerns have been rattling the global economy, forcing central banks of major nations to curtail any rate-cut moves.

Yesterday, Trump warned that if Iran fails to lift off the blockade to the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. forces would decimate all power and energy installations and oil refineries in Iran and capture Kharg Island.

In the same note, Trump also acknowledged that the U.S. was engaged in serious discussions with reliable mediators to end the conflict.

In addition, more U.S. troops were deployed to the Middle East over the past few days. As war-threat increased, oil prices went up north.

Today, citing officials within the U.S. administration, the Wall Street Journal reported that Trump expressed willingness to end the military campaign against Iran and instead pressure Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz using diplomatic methods and was ready to let U.S. allies in Europe and the Gulf region to take up the responsibility of reopening the Strait of Hormuz if U.S.-Iran diplomacy fails.

This report from the WSJ indicating an end to the U.S. role in the current phase of Middle East war took off some heat.

Market participants shed the war jitters, leading to a cool-off in oil prices.

However, the ground-level situation remained tense. In an overnight attack, an oil tanker Al-Salmi connected to Kuwait and anchored off Dubai was struck by an Iranian drone attack.

Saudi Arabia intercepted two drones from Iran.

Meanwhile, the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran in the Isfahan province targeted some 'military sites.'

In an interview with Bloomberg today, Fereidun Fesharaki, Chairman Emeritus of the energy consultancy firm FGE NexantECA remarked that if the Middle East situation does not improve in the next six to eight weeks, oil prices could jump to $200 per barrel and even higher if the Strait of Hormuz remains near-closed as it is at the moment.

In the U.S., the average price of gasoline at the pump has topped $4.00 for the first time in nearly four years, with the national average hovering around $4.02.

Similarly, in the United Kingdom, average petrol prices have increased by 14.00% and diesel prices have surged by 27.00% since the war began.

Elsewhere globally, governments are struggling to keep fuel prices under control. Economists warn that a prolonged crisis could prompt discretionary spending cuts.

The U.S. dollar index was last seen trading at 99.99, down by 0.57 points (or 0.57%) today.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: U.S. Oil Output May Be Hitting a Wall at a Bad Time — WSJ
data.description:
By David Uberti

Don't count on American shale to come to the rescue if this oil shock lasts.

American drillers pumped 13.2 million barrels of oil a day in January, according to new data from the Energy Information Administration, down from 13.9 million in October. Outside of the pandemic, the three-month decline marks one of the largest such pullbacks in almost a decade.

Weekly data suggests that moderately lower production has continued in recent weeks.

While a boom in U.S. output acted as a shock absorber for higher oil prices after Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, analysts warn that plateauing production now could exacerbate the impact of Iran's de facto blockade of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Lithium South Announces Closing Date of April 7, 2026
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / Lithium South Development Corporation (the "Company" or "Lithium South") (TSX-V:LIS)(OTCQB:LISMF)(Frankfurt:OGPQ) is pleased to announce that it has arranged a closing date of April 7, 2026 for the arm's length asset sale (the "Transaction") of its 100% owned Hombre Muerto North Lithium Project package (the ''HMN Li Package") as approved by shareholders on February 19, 2026 and previously announced on July 30, 2025, August 7, 2025, November 20, 2025, December 8, 2025 and February 4, 2026. The Transaction is for the sale of 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of the Company's wholly-owned subsidiary, NRG Metals Argentina S.A., (which holds title to the "HMN Li Package") with 99% to POSCO Argentina S.A.U. and 1% to POSCO Holdings Inc.

The effective date of the payout of the cash consideration of CAD$0.505 per common share pursuant to the Plan of Arrangement is expected to be finalized shortly and the shareholders will be apprised accordingly. The Company will be making applications to delist from the TSX Venture Exchange and the OTCQB market, and an application to cease being a reporting issuer.

The Company wishes to remind its Registered Shareholders that they are required to complete and return a letter of transmittal (the "Letter of Transmittal") to the Company's transfer agent, Computershare Investor Services Inc., in order to receive the cash consideration for their common shares pursuant to the Plan of Arrangement. The Letter of Transmittal may be retrieved from the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca posted on January 29, 2026, or contact Computershare by phone at 1-800-564-6253 or by email at corporateactions@computershare.com. Further details can also be found at Investors | Lithium South (TSX-V: LIS | OTCQB: LISMF)

Non-Registered shares held with financial intermediaries will be handled by the institution in coordination with Computershare Trust Company.

On behalf of the Board of Directors

Adrian F. C. Hobkirk

President and Chief Executive Officer

Investors / Shareholders call 855-415-8100 / website: www.lithiumsouth.com

data.title: Colombia central bank raises rate by 100 basis points amid spat with finance minister
data.description:
Colombia's central bank raised its benchmark interest rate for the second consecutive time on Tuesday, to 11.25%, in a bid to curb inflationary pressures that are keeping prices significantly above target.

The decision came after Finance Minister German Avila, who represents the government on the country's central bank board, said the government had withdrawn from the board until policymakers understand the need for coherence between their decisions and the economic reality of Colombia. Avila added that a 100-basis point rise was "disproportionate."

The increase was backed by four policymakers on the seven-member board, board chief Leonardo Villar said, while two policymakers voted for a 50 basis point cut and one for a hold. Villar told journalists he rejected accusations by Avila that board members were acting in their own interests and that they are doing everything they can to return inflation to its long-term 3% target next year. He added that Avila's withdrawal goes against constitutional norms.

data.title: Miner Acquisition Marks Significant Win for Trump Admin — WSJ
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: In Win for Trump, U.S. Firm Acquires Huge Congolese Cobalt Miner — WSJ
data.description:
By Alexandra Wexler

A U.S. firm has finally acquired one of the world's largest cobalt producers not already in China's hands, marking a significant win for the Trump administration.

The purchase brings to a close an effort, stretching back to the Biden administration, to secure critical cobalt supplies in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The buyer, Virtus Minerals, is venturing where other American companies often feared to tread, purchasing a company saddled with some $1 billion in debt, facing an incursion of thousands of informal workers at its richest mine, Mutoshi, and located in one of the world's toughest countries in which to do business.

On Friday, Virtus finalized its acquisition of the company, Chemaf, for $30 million and a commitment to raise some $720 million in investment, a leg-up for Washington in the race against Beijing for critical minerals in resource-rich Congo. Chemaf's assets are capable of producing around 5% of the world's cobalt, a metal used in jet fighters, mobile phones and electric-vehicle batteries. Virtus says it plans to sell all of future production to American or "U.S.-aligned" buyers.

Finding a U.S. company to purchase Chemaf proved a monumental task. The Biden administration repeatedly sought to interest American companies in the family-run copper-and-cobalt producer. But U.S. firms proved reluctant due to the company's tarnished reputation, in addition to Congo's poor infrastructure, limited skilled labor, resource nationalism and reputation for government corruption.

Chemaf's founder, Shiraz Virji, son of a Zanzibar spice merchant, once hired dozens of Gurkhas, Nepalese soldiers with a reputation for fierceness, to guard one of the company's mines against incursions by informal miners. Until 2018, children worked at Mutoshi alongside adults, according to the mine's general manager at the time and local miners. Chemaf also paid bribes at times, Christian Schöppe, then acting chief executive of Chemaf, told The Wall Street Journal in 2018.

The U.S. finally found a horse to back in Virtus, an eight-person Delaware-registered firm focused on national security-related investments. The company was founded in 2022 by a former Green Beret with supply-chain and logistics expertise, Phil Braun, and by a graduate of the U.S. Naval Academy and Harvard Business School, Andrew Powch, who has worked in private equity and at McKinsey.

"We are grateful for the support of both governments, and particularly Presidents Trump and [Félix] Tshisekedi for their shared vision of a stronger U.S.-DRC relationship," Braun, who is the Virtus CEO, told The Wall Street Journal. "Virtus and its partners are ready to get to work restoring jobs, revitalizing production and delivering on the promise of U.S.-DRC critical minerals trade."

The U.S. government has been "fully supportive" of the Virtus acquisition of Chemaf, according to a State Department official, who said "this is a priority project."

The official said it could demonstrate to the Congolese government that U.S. investment can deliver concrete benefits for the country's economy.

Virtus, in partnership with Lloyds Metals and Energy, an Indian mining company that will be Virtus' operating partner, is contributing an initial $200 million to the Chemaf deal, with another $475 million in financing coming from New York-based investment firm Orion Resource Partners and $75 million in other financing. Orion declined to comment.

Virtus has also reached a deal for an undisclosed sum with Chemaf's largest creditor, Trafigura, a commodity-trading giant based in Singapore and Switzerland, according to Powch, who is chief financial officer at Virtus. In 2022, Trafigura arranged a $600 million syndicated loan to expand minerals-processing capacity and finish mechanizing Chemaf's Mutoshi mine.

Chemaf currently holds about $1 billion of debt, including money owed to in-country contractors, according to people close to the company. Virtus declined to comment on any debt-settlement plans with local contractors.

After cobalt prices crashed in 2023, Chemaf's founder put the company up for sale. About $250 million to $300 million in capital expenditure is still needed to finish infrastructure upgrades and increase production to 75,000 tons of copper and 15,000 tons of cobalt annually, according to Powch.

"Our priority has been to support a credible solution that meets the expectations of all stakeholders and puts these assets on a sustainable footing — protecting jobs, delivering local economic benefits, and ensuring the continued production of critical minerals in the DRC," a Trafigura spokesman said.

It has at times been a difficult deal to bring to fruition thanks to Chemaf's previous business practices and debts. When the Journal visited the Mutoshi mine in 2018, Congolese miners working for the company could be seen descending into hand-dug shafts without helmets, shoes or safety equipment. Miners were using picks, shovels and bare hands to unearth rocks rich with metal. Water sometimes rushed into holes and drowned workers, and an earth mover buried one alive, local miners and company officials said at the time.

A project to address the issues associated with the informal miners formalized much of Mutoshi's work shortly after that visit, but those efforts were halted due to Covid. The mine is again host to informal miners working in unsafe conditions, according to Virtus executives.

Over more than a decade, Chinese companies, willing to take on significant risks, have spent billions of dollars buying up mines in Congo, which produces nearly 75% of the world's cobalt.

"It would be very, very irresponsible for Western companies to continue to wait until we have a peaceful country," Guy Robert Lukama, former chairman of Gécamines, Congo's state-owned mining company, told the Journal. "We are not Sweden, and we'll never be Sweden."

Congolese officials didn't respond to requests for comment.

The Chinese coveted Chemaf as well. In June 2024, Chemaf agreed to sell itself to Norin Mining, a subsidiary of Chinese state-owned defense company Norinco, for $920 million. That deal would have seen all creditors fully paid, according to a person close to the deal.

The Norin deal fell apart last year because it couldn't secure the approvals it needed from the Congolese. Shortly after, Virtus appeared on the scene, touting its U.S. government backing, according to the person close to the deal.

But some in the industry question whether Virtus can actually get the mines up and running, even if it has acquired them on paper. Virtus invested in a copper-and-cobalt processing plant near Lubumbashi, Congo, in 2023. But that investment remains in limbo due to a title dispute, according to Virtus.

And although Lloyds operates mines in India and elsewhere, and has been leasing out mining equipment in Congo since 2018, the company's experience lies mainly in iron ore, bauxite and coal in Asia.

Soorya Narayanan Prabhakaran, a director of Lloyds, says the company has contracting experience in copper, and previously operated a mine in Mozambique, in southern Africa. He expects to commence work at Chemaf this month and predicts the $250 million to $300 million in upgrades to Chemaf's operations should be completed by the beginning of next year.

Write to Alexandra Wexler at alexandra.wexler@wsj.com

data.title: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac Stock Dip After Best Day in More Than a Decade — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Shaina Mishkin

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac stock on Monday notched their largest one-day increases in over a decade after traders took investor Bill Ackman's advice.

Ackman, founder of the hedge fund Pershing Square Capital Management, said on the social-media website X on Sunday that "some of the highest quality businesses in the world are trading at extremely cheap prices."

"And Fannie and Freddie are stupidly cheap. Asymmetry at its best," he wrote in a follow-up post. "They could be a 10X and it could happen soon."

The stocks took off on Monday: Fannie Mae closed 51% higher, logging its largest one-day gain since August 2009, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Freddie Mac gained 47%, its largest gain since March 2013. Both were rising in Tuesday trading.

It was only the latest fluctuation as investors await signs that the Trump administration will work to remove the companies from their long-running conservatorship or sell shares in a new offering. Despite the one-day increase, both are down this year, and are roughly 50% lower than their 52-week highs in September.

"The last 24 hours, we think, is an Ackman-driven push," says Eric Hagen, who covers the stocks for BTIG. "I wouldn't point to anything really fundamental."

Ackman's hedge fund has large positions in both companies' common shares, according to FactSet. The investor's post on X follows a Barron's report earlier this month that he recently met with Trump administration officials to advocate for retiring the companies' "senior" preferred shares, which represent some of the government's claim on the companies.

There doesn't appear to be any news on administrative action that changes the prospects for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac investors. The Federal Housing Finance Agency, the White House, Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac didn't immediately respond to Barron's request for comment. Pershing Square declined to comment.

Hagen, the BTIG analyst, values the stocks at $20 each. Fannie Mae is at $7.88 in Tuesday trading. Freddie Mac is at $6.92.

His price target hinges on political action. "We agree with Bill Ackman and we buy into the same argument that some simple procedural changes regarding the senior preferred stock and the level of capital — the capital ratios — would clear the way for a total rerating in the stocks, " Hagen says. "The likelihood of that happening — it changes every day."

Write to Shaina Mishkin at shaina.mishkin@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Citi is a prime candidate to revive big-bank M&A
data.description:
By Stephen Gandel

Though it denies any interest, the Wall Street giant would benefit from a major tie-up. Extra scale should help slash the high cost of its deposits, while regulators are as amenable as they’ve been since 2008. Combining with regional lender Truist could generate $40 bln in value.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Stephen Gandel on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

Citigroup executives are considering whether they should buy a regional bank, Bloomberg reported on March 27. The bank's executives have reached out to regulators, who were receptive to the idea of an acquisition, according to the report.

Citi dismissed the report, saying in a statement to Reuters that “the suggestion that Citi is planning to buy a regional bank, wealth brokerage - or any other financial services firm - is baseless speculation.”

data.title: Why This Pharma Stock More Than Doubled Today — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares in small biopharmaceutical firm Apellis Pharmaceuticals more than doubled in Tuesday trading after Biogen said it would acquire the company for $5.6 billion. It's a big boost for a stock that had shed nearly a third of its value this year.

The commercial-stage biopharma company, which focuses on rare diseases, struck a deal to be acquired by Biogen on Tuesday. Under the terms of the arrangement, Biogen will pay $41 a share for all shares outstanding of Apellis — a steep premium to the stock's closing price of $17.09 on Monday.

Apellis stockholders will also receive a contingent value right for each share they own. This gives them the right to collect two extra payments of $2 a share each, provided that global sales of Syfovre, the company's geographic atrophy treatment, hit specific yearly targets.

It might seem like Biogen is paying a lot to acquire the smaller company, and Tuesday's stock moves suggest investors weren't too pleased. As Apellis surged 136% to $40.26, marking its largest daily percent gain on record, Biogen fell 3.3%. The benchmark S&P 500 was 2.5% higher.

Shares of Apellis have floundered lately. Heading into Tuesday, the stock had fallen 32% this year and nearly 25% over the past 12 months. Its protracted slump has only been compounded by recent risk-off sentiment amid the war in Iran, causing investors to flee speculative biotech stocks.

Apellis posted a 6% decline in total revenue for the fourth quarter. Syfovre sales alone dropped 8% from same period last year as a competing treatment from drugmaker Astellas continued to chip away at its market share.

Analysts have flagged concerns over the flat growth trajectory of Apellis' products. The company currently has two drugs on the market: Syfovre and Empaveli. The latter is approved to treat two rare kidney diseases.

Biogen stock, meanwhile, has gained 6.6% this year and 42% over the past 12 months, partly driven by better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter. The standout in its portfolio is Leqembi, an Alzheimer's drug co-developed with Eisai, which has shaped into Biogen's newest blockbuster medicine.

The S&P 500 has declined 7.3% this year and gained 13% over the past 12 months, meaning Biogen has outpaced the broader market over both periods. That's not to say the company isn't facing its own problems. Its aging multiple sclerosis franchise continues to suffer losses that must be offset by newer drugs.

If Biogen is looking for winners to add to its portfolio, the key could be Syfovre and Empaveli. Biogen expects the inclusion of both products to boost revenue immediately. The therapies brought in $689 million in combined net sales last year. Biogen added that it expects revenue is to grow at a mid- to high-teens percent through at least 2028.

The acquisition aligns with Biogen's focus on breathing life into its burgeoning growth portfolio, with a particular focus here on immunology and rare disease. Revenue for the latter sank 16% in the fourth quarter. The deal will also accelerate the company's expansion into nephrology, which began in late 2024 with the acquisition of Human Immunology Biosciences.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Centaurus Announces Grant of Options
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - March 31, 2026) - Centaurus Energy Inc. (OTCID: CTARF) ("Centaurus" or the "Company"), announces the grant of 54,403 options to David Tawil, Chairman and CEO, pursuant to the Company's share option plan, exercisable at a price of USD$1.25 per share and expiring on March 30, 2031.

The grants were made in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Company's share option plan (the "Share Option Plan") that was approved by the shareholders of the Company on February 26, 2025. The Share Options Plan is a "rolling" 10% plan that provides for the grant of stock options ("Options"). The aggregate number of common shares reserved for issuance under the Share Option Plan, at any time, must not exceed 10% of the Company's total issued and outstanding common shares. For additional information regarding the Share Option Plan, please refer to the Company's management information circular dated January 24, 2025, which is accessible on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca) under the Company's issuer profile.

About Centaurus

Centaurus is a company focused on investing in physical and digital commodities. The Company's shares trade on the TSX-V under the symbol "CTA" and on the OTC ID Market under the symbol "CTARF".

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASE CONTACT:

David Tawil, Chairman & Chief Executive Officer

email: davidtawil@ctaurus.com

phone: (646) 479-9387

data.title: Vycor Medical 10-K: Revenue $1.86M, EPS (0.01) — FY Summary
data.description:
Vycor Medical reported fiscal year revenue of $1.8634 million and a net loss per share of $(0.01), reflecting a modest operating profit but a small net loss after preferred dividends. Revenue benefited from an 18% increase in Vycor product sales while overall net loss available to common stockholders widened to $(396,796). Gross margins remained strong overall, supporting operating income improvement versus the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1.8634 million (Vycor Medical product revenue up 18% to $1.79607 million; NovaVision $67,330).
  • Gross Profit: $1.561107 million; overall gross margin approximately 84% (Vycor ~83%; NovaVision ~93%).
  • Operating Income: $33,900 (improved from an operating loss of $9,145 in the prior year).
  • Net Loss (Net Income): $(72,426); net loss available to common stockholders $(396,796) after preferred dividends.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $(0.01) basic and diluted (weighted average shares outstanding 33,372,796).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Vycor product revenue rose 18% year-over-year, driven primarily by increased international demand, particularly in Europe.
  • Geographic Mix: Majority of the sales increase came from international markets, shifting the company’s sales mix toward exports and increasing related logistics and validation activities.
  • Channel and Operational Changes: The sales lift was largely due to channel shifts toward international distributors and customers, which also contributed to higher related costs.
  • Brand Performance: NovaVision sales declined modestly (down 8%) to $67K but continued to produce a high gross margin (~93%).
  • Production and Cost Dynamics: Vycor experienced gross margin compression (83% vs. prior 89%) attributable to product validation, increased shipping costs and new production-related expenses.
  • R&D and SG&A Focus: R&D spending moderated for early-stage Vycor development while SG&A increased, driven by investor relations activity and stock-based compensation.

Original SEC Filing: VYCOR MEDICAL INC [ VYCO ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Investview 2025 10-K: Revenue $36.3M, Net Loss $8.9M
data.description:
Investview reported total revenue of $36.26 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, a 31% decline year-over-year driven by lower membership and mining revenues, and recorded a net loss of $8.91 million for the period. The filing does not disclose basic or diluted net income per share in the provided excerpts. Operational shifts and acquisitions during the year accompanied the weaker top-line performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue, net: $36.26 million, decreased $16.13 million (31%) vs 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $28.01 million (as stated in the summary).
  • Operating Income: Operating costs and expenses $44.516 million versus revenue $36.256 million (operating loss implied in summary).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $8.908 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not stated in the provided excerpts.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and decline: The revenue decline was driven by a 38% drop in membership revenue and a 36% decline in mining revenue, reflecting weaker demand and operational headwinds in those segments.
  • Channel and product pivot: The company redirected direct-to-consumer efforts toward health and wellness products and completed the acquisition of Renu Labs, which added a manufacturing channel and contributed approximately $3.6 million in product sales.
  • Acquisitions and platform expansion: Investview acquired Opencash, a broker-dealer platform, and completed the Renu Labs acquisition, including stock option incentives to management as part of the transaction structure.
  • Mining operations: Mining output was reduced by industry and regional factors — notably the Bitcoin halving, higher network difficulty, and regional power curtailments — despite higher BTC prices.
  • Regulatory and legal matters: The company settled an SEC inquiry, is appealing a PLN fine in Poland, and is pursuing litigation related to TPP affecting customer programs.

Original SEC Filing: Investview, Inc. [ INVU ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: First Guaranty Bancshares 2025 10‑K: $(56.0)M Net Loss, EPS $(4.17), NII $86.9M
data.description:
First Guaranty Bancshares reported a net loss of $56.0 million for the year ended 2025, driven by elevated credit loss provisions and a $12.9 million goodwill impairment; basic loss per share was $4.17. Net interest income totaled $86.9 million with a net interest margin of 2.28%, down from 2.47% in 2024. The results reflect a de-risking strategy that materially reduced loans while expanding liquid securities and altering the deposit mix.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $86.9 million; net interest margin 2.28% (2025) vs 2.47% (2024).
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(56.0) million for 2025, a decrease of $68.5 million versus 2024 (impacted by credit loss provisions and a $12.9 million goodwill impairment).
  • Net (Loss) Income Available to Common Shareholders: $(58.35) million; basic EPS $(4.17) for 2025 vs $0.81 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Loan Portfolio Shift: Executed a de-risking strategy in 2025 that reduced loan balances by approximately 23% through payoffs, sales and targeted charge-offs, reflecting a deliberate reduction of risk exposure.
  • Deposit & Liquidity Mix: Deposits grew about 4.5% with a shift toward time and brokered deposits; the company increased holdings of cash and liquid securities to bolster liquidity.
  • Securities & Liquidity Build: Investment portfolio expanded significantly, notably with increased mortgage-backed securities to support liquidity and collateral requirements.
  • Asset Quality Actions: Increased workout activity including higher charge-offs and acquisitions of other real estate owned (OREO); management identified focused monitoring for commercial real estate, healthcare and hospitality exposures.
  • Branch/Market Move: Executed an agreement to sell Texas operations—five branches with roughly $270 million in deposits and about $110 million in loans—to streamline the franchise footprint.

Original SEC Filing: First Guaranty Bancshares, Inc. [ FGBI ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: BranchOut 2025 10-K: $13.7M Revenue, $(0.57) EPS
data.description:
BranchOut reported net revenue of $13.7 million for 2025, a 113% increase year-over-year driven by higher sales volumes and new product introductions, while net loss per share was $(0.57) on a weighted average of 10,734,295 shares. Despite improved net margin, the company recorded a net loss of $(6.1247) million and an operating loss of $(5.3635) million as operating expenses rose during scale-up. Gross profit was $2.0344 million with an adjusted gross profit of $3.8201 million reflecting margin improvements from increased internal production.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $13.7246 million, 113% increase vs 2024.
  • Gross profit: $2.0344 million; gross margin 14.8%.
  • Adjusted gross profit: $3.8201 million; adjusted gross margin 27.8%.
  • Operating loss: $(5.3635) million, increased from $(3.9024) million in 2024.
  • Net loss: $(6.1247) million, compared to $(4.7515) million in 2024; net margin (44.6)% (improved from (73.8)%).
  • Net loss per common share — basic and diluted: $(0.57); weighted average shares 10,734,295.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Higher sales volumes and launches of new BranchOut products and private-label offerings expanded revenue in 2025.
  • Channel and production shift: Transitioned manufacturing from third-party providers to in-house production at the Peru facility, with ramp-up beginning December 2024.
  • Manufacturing scale & efficiency: Peru facility scale-up increased the share of internal production, improving throughput and contributing to better gross margins.
  • Brand momentum and distribution: Expanded distribution with large national retail customers and broadened product assortment through new launches and private-label contracts.
  • Operational milestones: Completed build-out of the Peru facility and incurred start-up and idle capacity costs while optimizing production and logistics during scale-up.

Original SEC Filing: BranchOut Food Inc. [ BOF ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ocean Re financial strength rating upgraded to A by AM Best
data.description:
By Chris Munro

Ocean International Reinsurance Company has had its financial strength rating upgraded to A from A-minus by AM Best, with the agency citing the Latin America-focused reinsurer’s “disciplined underwriting” and “strong” operating performance metrics for the change.

Barbados-domiciled Ocean Re offers a diversified product mix worldwide “with a relevant presence in Latin America,” AM Best said.

Ocean Re, which is led by CEO Guillermo Eslava, also writes facultative reinsurance programs alongside its treaty offerings.

As well as the FSR improvement, AM Best upgraded Ocean Re’s long-term issuer credit rating to a from a-minus.

In a statement, AM Best said the rating upgrades “recognize the disciplined underwriting by Ocean Re to deploy its capital with profitable results.”

“The company’s operating performance metrics are strong, in conjunction with growth targets based on its diversified geographical exposures, new business and successful growth of the risk-bearing portfolio,” it added.

Its risk-adjusted capitalization stands at the strongest level, as measured by Best’s Capital Adequacy Ratio, and the reinsurer has benefited from profitable results up to September 30, 2025, said AM Best.

At the same time, Ocean Re’s regional geographic footprint has continued to expand, with the reinsurer present in 86 countries as of December 2024, with the ability to further diversify throughout Latin America, Asia, the Middle East and North Africa, among other regions.

The upgrades also reflect Ocean Re’s “very strong” balance sheet strength, its neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management.

data.title: SSL: Net loss of €20.37M, equity up to €103.21M after €140.41M asset revaluation
data.description:
Net loss of €20.37 million driven by lower revenues from missing European competitions and higher costs, partially offset by a €140.41 million asset revaluation. Net financial debt rose to €111.31 million, while equity increased to €103.21 million.

data.title: With Prospective Plantings Out, Grain Traders Turn to Weather — Market Talk
data.description: Now that the USDA has published its projections for how much corn, soybeans, and wheat will be planted this spring, the focus among traders is turning toward the weather, says Brian Grete of Commstock Investments. "Weather will be a larger focus with the flip of the calendar to April," says Grete. "We'll start adding/subtracting acres based on weather, market prices, fertilizer prices/availability, etc." But Grete notes that with war headlines still dominating the news cycle, trader focus on fundamentals for grains may be limited. Late in trading, corn is up 0.2%, soybeans rise 1%, and wheat is up 1.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)
